findings.evidence: docs/requirements.md's FR-10 scenario was changed from '...a real committed artifact (NFR-4)' to '...a real committed artifact — matching the product brief's report-format NFR (JSON/markdown, versioned)'. NFR-4 in docs/PRODUCT-BRIEF.md reads 'The report format SHALL default to versioned JSON/markdown.' But the actual generated artifact docs/reports/dogfood-self-eval.json, and the Report/RunReport shapes in build-report.ts, contain no version/schemaVersion field at all — just { generatedAt, runs: [...] }. A reader using the requirements doc to judge whether the shipped report satisfies NFR-4 would be told 'versioned' is matched when the artifact itself has nothing that could be checked as a version.

docs/vocabulary.md's Epic 2b section and docs/plan.md name the concept 'DogfoodAdapter' ('the identity-case adapter for this harness's own telemetry'), and load.spec.ts's describe block says 'FR-1: DogfoodAdapter loads and validates its own telemetry...'. But grepping the package for 'DogfoodAdapter' finds it nowhere as an actual exported symbol — load.ts only exports a bare function loadDogfoodRun. This is inconsistent with the sibling KeystoneAdapter, which is materialized as a literal exported const object (packages/ai-factory-kaizen/src/adapters/keystone/index.ts: 'export const KeystoneAdapter = {...}', explicitly justified as 'the single typed representation of docs/vocabulary.md's KeystoneAdapter term'). A future reader grepping the code for the vocabulary term 'DogfoodAdapter' will not find it.

The persisted report mixes naming conventions within the same object: top-level/run fields use snake_case (harness_id, spec_id) while the nested metrics object uses camelCase (buildTestPassed, requirementCoveragePercent, cycleTimeSeconds). E.g. docs/reports/dogfood-self-eval.json has both "harness_id": "ai-factory-kaizen-dogfood" and "buildTestPassed": true in the same run entry. This mirrors the existing EvalRunResult/DerivedMetrics domain split, but surfacing it unchanged in a human-readable report artifact (this diff's stated purpose) makes the file read as two different styles stitched together rather than one coherent report shape.
